project.name: ft_irc
project.title: Internet Relay Chat
project.summary: Create your own IRC server in C++98 and test it using a real IRC client.

introduction.description: IRC is a text-based real-time communication protocol allowing public and private messaging via channels. Clients connect to servers, and servers form networks.

constraints: Program must never crash or quit unexpectedly.
Provide a Makefile without unnecessary relinking.
Makefile rules required: NAME, all, clean, fclean, re.
Prefer C++ standard libraries over C equivalents.
External libraries and Boost are forbidden.

guidelines: Only use AI-generated content you fully understand.
Always verify and peer-review AI output.
good_practices: Use AI for ideas, then validate with peers.
Design with AI assistance but review logic collaboratively.
bad_practices: Fail to explain AI-generated logic during evaluation.

notes: Do not develop an IRC client.
Equivalent to poll() allowed: select(), kqueue(), epoll().

core: Only one poll() (or equivalent) for all operations.
networking: Reference IRC client required for evaluation.
mandatory_features: Broadcast channel messages to all members.

rules: Allowed fcntl usage: fcntl(fd, F_SETFL, O_NONBLOCK);

notes: Aggregate packets to rebuild full commands.

bonus_part.condition: Mandatory part must be perfectly completed.

evaluation: Possible small live modification during defense.
Must justify and explain implementation clearly.
